independent continuant c

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000004
Description

A continuant that is a bearer of quality and realizable entity entities, in which other entities inhere and which itself cannot inhere in anything.
Sub Class Of continuant c
In Domain Of
  • has function op
  • has role op
  • has disposition op
  • capable of op
Restriction part of op only independent continuant c
Super Class Of material entity c

process c

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000015
Description

An occurrent that has temporal proper parts and for some time t, p s-depends_on some material entity at t.

specifically dependent continuant c

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000020
Is Defined By obo:bfo.owl
Description

A continuant that inheres in or is borne by other entities. Every instance of A requires some specific instance of B which must always be the same.
Sub Class Of continuant c
In Range Of has characteristic op
Restriction part of op only specifically dependent continuant c
Super Class Of

  • quality c
  • quality c

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_3200074

gene product or complex activitymolecular_function c

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0003674
Description

Note that, in addition to forming the root of the molecular function ontology, this term is recommended for the annotation of gene products whose molecular function is unknown. When this term is used for annotation, it indicates that no information was available about the molecular function of the gene product annotated as of the date the annotation was made; the evidence code 'no data' (ND), is used to indicate this. Despite its name, this is not a type of 'function' in the sense typically defined by upper ontologies such as Basic Formal Ontology (BFO). It is instead a BFO:process carried out by a single gene product or complex.
Sub Class Of process c
Super Class Of kinase activity c

biological processbiological_process c

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0008150
Description

Note that, in addition to forming the root of the biological process ontology, this term is recommended for the annotation of gene products whose biological process is unknown. When this term is used for annotation, it indicates that no information was available about the biological process of the gene product annotated as of the date the annotation was made; the evidence code 'no data' (ND), is used to indicate this.
Sub Class Of process c

kinase activity c

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0016301
Description

Note that this term encompasses all activities that transfer a single phosphate group; although ATP is by far the most common phosphate donor, reactions using other phosphate donors are included in this term.
Sub Class Of gene product or complex activity c
Restriction is kinase activity op

curation status specification c

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000078
Equivalentclass

quality c

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0000001
Sub Class Of specifically dependent continuant c
In Range Of regulates characteristic op
Super Class Of
  • physical object quality c
  • quantitative c

concentration of c

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0000033
Sub Class Of amount c
Super Class Of osmolarity c

amount c

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0000070
Description

This term was originally named "presence". It has been renamed to reduce ambiguity. Consider annotating with the reciprocal relation,PATO:0001555, has_number_of. For example, rather than E=fin ray Q=count in organism C=10, say E=organism Q=has number of E2= fin ray C=10.
Sub Class Of quantitative c
Restriction reciprocal of op some has number of c
Super Class Of
  • concentration of c
  • has number of c

physical quality c

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001018
Sub Class Of physical object quality c
Super Class Of
  • environmental condition c
  • cation exchange capacity c

physical object quality c

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001241
Description

Relational qualities are qualities that hold between multiple entities. Normal (monadic) qualities such as the shape of a eyeball exist purely as a quality of that eyeball. A relational quality such as sensitivity to light is a quality of that eyeball (and connecting nervous system) as it relates to incoming light waves/particles.
Sub Class Of quality c
Super Class Of physical quality c

has number of c

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001555
Sub Class Of amount c

osmolarity c

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0001655
Sub Class Of concentration of c
Super Class Of salinity c

salinity c

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0085001
Sub Class Of osmolarity c

quantitative c

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/PATO_0103000
Sub Class Of quality c
Super Class Of amount c

root node c

rewetting process c

Description

Sub Class Of mechanical hydrological alteration process c

site preparation process c

IRI https://w3id.org/elmo/elmo_3621053
Sub Class Of mechanical ecosystem management process c
Super Class Of soil scarification process c

soil scarification process c

IRI https://w3id.org/elmo/elmo_3621054
Description

Distinct from seed scarification process (ELMO:3620617), which refers to mechanical abrasion of seed coats to break dormancy. In restoration literature the term 'scarification' often refers to this soil-level treatment.
Sub Class Of site preparation process c

transplanting process c

IRI https://w3id.org/elmo/elmo_3621055
Sub Class Of plant material transfer process c

straw mulch addition process c

Sub Class Of fertilizer application process c

IRI https://w3id.org/elmo/elmo_3621058
Sub Class Of fertilizer application process c

peat extraction process c

IRI https://w3id.org/elmo/elmo_3621059
Description

Represents a disturbance driver that typically necessitates subsequent restoration interventions such as peatland rewetting process (ELMO:3620646) and moss layer transfer process (ELMO:3620631).
Sub Class Of mechanical ecosystem management process c

fire return interval c

IRI https://w3id.org/elmo/elmo_3622601
Description

Distinct from fire frequency, which counts the number of fire events per unit time; the two are inversely related.

Sub Class Of plant recruitment variable c

water table depth c

IRI https://w3id.org/elmo/elmo_3622713
Description

Positive values indicate the water table is below ground surface; a value of zero indicates the water table is at the surface; negative values indicate inundation. Also reported as water table level relative to a fixed datum.
Sub Class Of hydrological and water quality variable c

light availability c

IRI https://w3id.org/elmo/elmo_3622714
Sub Class Of climatic and meteorological variable c

Sphagnum cover c

IRI https://w3id.org/elmo/elmo_3622715
Description

Sphagnum cover is the standard measured quantity at a point in time; the term 'Sphagnum regeneration' is commonly used in the peatland restoration literature to refer to the same phenomenon, particularly when framing it as a post-restoration outcome.
Sub Class Of vegetation structure variable c

microbial biomass c

IRI https://w3id.org/elmo/elmo_3622716
Sub Class Of productivity and biogeochemical variable c

vegetation cover c

IRI https://w3id.org/elmo/elmo_3622717
Description

Encompasses canopy cover (ELMO:3622302), shrub cover (ELMO:3622309), herbaceous cover (ELMO:3622310), and understory cover (ELMO:3622308) as narrower terms. Use this term when reporting total across-strata plant cover.
Sub Class Of vegetation structure variable c

competition intensity c

IRI https://w3id.org/elmo/elmo_3622718
Sub Class Of disturbance and pressure variable c

time since restoration c

regeneration failure rate c

Sub Class Of ecological process variable c

habitat loss area c

Sub Class Of disturbance and pressure variable c

seismic line density c

IRI https://w3id.org/elmo/elmo_3622805
Description

Seismic lines are a key disturbance type in Canadian boreal forest and peatland restoration, particularly in Alberta.

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_02000200
Sub Property Of has component op

characteristic of op

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052
Description

Note that this relation was previously called "inheres in", but was changed to be called "characteristic of" because BFO2 uses "inheres in" in a more restricted fashion. This relation differs from BFO2:inheres_in in two respects: (1) it does not impose a range constraint, and thus it allows qualities of processes, as well as of information entities, whereas BFO2 restricts inheres_in to only apply to independent continuants (2) it is declared functional, i.e. something can only be a characteristic of one thing.
Sub Property Of characteristic of part of op
Super Property Of
  • function of op
  • quality of op
  • role of op
  • disposition of op

has characteristic op

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000053
Super Property Of
  • has function op
  • has quality op
  • has role op
  • has disposition op
Range specifically dependent continuant c

  • involved in op
  • input of op
Domain continuant c

has participant op

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000057
Source http://www.obofoundry.org/ro/#OBO_REL:has_participant
Super Property Of
  • has input op
  • enabled by op
Domain occurrent c
Range continuant c

function of op

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000079
Description

This relation is modeled after the BFO relation of the same name which was in BFO2, but is used in a more restricted sense - specifically, we model this relation as functional (inherited from characteristic-of). Note that this relation is now removed from BFO2020.
Sub Property Of characteristic of op
Domain function c

quality of op

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000080
Description

This relation is modeled after the BFO relation of the same name which was in BFO2, but is used in a more restricted sense - specifically, we model this relation as functional (inherited from characteristic-of). Note that this relation is now removed from BFO2020.
Sub Property Of characteristic of op

Description

This relation is modeled after the BFO relation of the same name which was in BFO2, but is used in a more restricted sense - specifically, we model this relation as functional (inherited from characteristic-of). Note that this relation is now removed from BFO2020.

has regulatory component activity op

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002013
Sub Property Of
  • has component activity op
  • regulated by op
Super Property Of
  • has negative regulatory component activity op
  • has positive regulatory component activity op

has negative regulatory component activity op

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002014
Description

By convention GO molecular functions are classified by their effector function. Internal regulatory functions are treated as components. For example, NMDA glutmate receptor activity is a cation channel activity with positive regulatory component 'glutamate binding' and negative regulatory components including 'zinc binding' and 'magnesium binding'.
Sub Property Of
  • has regulatory component activity op
  • negatively regulated by op

has positive regulatory component activity op

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002015
Description

By convention GO molecular functions are classified by their effector function and internal regulatory functions are treated as components. So, for example calmodulin has a protein binding activity that has positive regulatory component activity calcium binding activity. Receptor tyrosine kinase activity is a tyrosine kinase activity that has positive regulatory component 'ligand binding'.
Sub Property Of
  • has regulatory component activity op
  • positively regulated by op

has component activity op

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002017
Description

A 'has component activity' B if A is A and B are molecular functions (GO_0003674) and A has_component B.
Sub Property Of has component process op
Super Property Of
  • has regulatory component activity op
  • has effector activity op

has component process op

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002018
Sub Property Of has component op
Super Property Of has component activity op
Domain process c
Range process c

directly regulated by op

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002022
Description

Process(P2) is directly regulated by process(P1) iff: P1 regulates P2 via direct physical interaction between an agent executing P1 (or some part of P1) and an agent executing P2 (or some part of P2). For example, if protein A has protein binding activity(P1) that targets protein B and this binding regulates the kinase activity (P2) of protein B then P1 directly regulates P2.
Sub Property Of regulated by op
Super Property Of
  • directly negatively regulated by op
  • directly positively regulated by op

directly negatively regulated by op

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002023
Sub Property Of directly regulated by op

directly positively regulated by op

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002024
Sub Property Of directly regulated by op

has effector activity op

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002025
Description

This relation is designed for constructing compound molecular functions, typically in combination with one or more regulatory component activity relations.
Sub Property Of has component activity op

Ro_0002084 op

ends after op

Description

X ends_after Y iff: end(Y) before_or_simultaneous_with end(X)

Description

reciprocal of op

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0015012
Description

There are frequently two ways to state the same thing: we can say 'spermatocyte lacks asters' or 'asters absent from spermatocyte'. In this case the quality is 'lacking all parts of type' - it is a (relational) quality of the spermatocyte, and it is with respect to instances of 'aster'. One of the popular requirements of PATO is that it continue to support 'absent', so we need to relate statements which use this quality to the 'lacking all parts of type' quality.

device utilizes material op

Functional Properties

characteristic of fp

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0000052
Description

Note that this relation was previously called "inheres in", but was changed to be called "characteristic of" because BFO2 uses "inheres in" in a more restricted fashion. This relation differs from BFO2:inheres_in in two respects: (1) it does not impose a range constraint, and thus it allows qualities of processes, as well as of information entities, whereas BFO2 restricts inheres_in to only apply to independent continuants (2) it is declared functional, i.e. something can only be a characteristic of one thing.
Sub Property Of characteristic of part of op
Super Property Of
  • function of op
  • quality of op
  • role of op
  • disposition of op

has effector activity fp

IRI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002025
Description

This relation is designed for constructing compound molecular functions, typically in combination with one or more regulatory component activity relations.
Sub Property Of has component activity op

Custom Datatypes

date dt

IRI http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#date
